elements.name: Description
texts: Gerald of Wales describes the number and disposition of Ireland's lakes

elements.name: Source
texts: Gerald of Wales: The History and Topography of Ireland, translated by John O'Meara, 1.4, p. 37

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "This country, above all others we have seen, is well supplied also with beautiful lakes, full of fish and very large. They are a kind of speciality here. There contains islands rising to some height and very beautiful. The lords of the land usually appropriate them as places of safety and refuge, as well as habitation. They are inaccessible except to boats."
